ocr.text: The following data table presents critical insights into the assessment of various infant formulas, specifically focusing on their
safety and efficacy in relation to trace metal contaminants and immunological responses. Notably, the metrics for cadmium (Cd)
and lead (Pb) levels are of particular concern due to their potential health implications for infants. For instance, the analysis of
Infant Formula B reveals cadmium concentrations of 0.103 ± 0.091 mg/kg, a level that underscores the importance of ongoing
monitoring and regulatory compliance to ensure infant safety. Additionally, the table highlights the efficacy of a vaccine
formulated using the Ebola_VP30/Baculovirus mechanism, which operates through a virus-like particle (VLP) strategy aimed at
eliciting an adaptive immune response in tested species, specifically mice. The efficacy results, alongside the percentages of
subjects exhibiting major depression and those without major depression, provide a comprehensive overview of both the
immunological outcomes and the psychological implications of the tested formulas. This multifaceted analysis is crucial for
informing healthcare providers and regulatory bodies about the potential risks and benefits associated with these infant feeding
options.
Infant formula code  Cd  Pb  Zn
Infant formula B  ND∗  0.103 ± 0.091  45.732 ± 0.555
Infant formula L  ND  0.051 ± 0.058  71.553 ± 1.104
Infant formula M  ND  0.016 ± 0.027  39.873 ± 0.921
Infant formula N  ND  0.063 ± 0.065  42.127 ± 1.014
Infant formula P  ND  ND  27.888 ± 0.542
Vaccine  Type  Mechanism  Species Tested  Efficacy  Strategy
Ebola_VP30/Baculovirus  Virus-like particle (VLP)  Adaptive Immune Response  Mice  Drug dependent-up to 100%  Multiple i.m. injections multiple boosts
EBOV-GP-Fc fusion protein  Fusion protein/ subunit vaccine  Adaptive Immune Response  Mice  Up to 90%  4 i.p. injections, ~3 weeks apart
Nicotiana Benthamiana  Subunit vaccine  Adaptive Immune Response  Mice  High specific antibody titer  4 s.c. injections, 3 weeks apart
mCMV/EBOV-NPCTL  CMV-based vaccine  Adaptive Immune Response  Mice  Drug dependent-up to 100%  2 i.p. injections, 4 weeks apart
HPIV3/ΔF-HN/EboGP  Vector-based vaccine, replication competent  Adaptive Immune Response  Guinea Pigs  100% EBOV  1 i.n. inoculation, 25 days prior to infection
n  Major Depression% (95 CI)  No Major Depression% (95 CI)
